meaning: adj.匆匆忙忙的,粗略的,草率的
examples: Burke cast a cursory glance at the menu, then flapped it shut.
Her technique remained awkward, thin, and often cursory.
Parkin's characterization of the movement as Neo-Scholastic is too cursory to be accepted without further investigation.
Even a cursory glance at Icahn's record shows he will respond to that incentive.
Speaking of languishing plot lines, we also get a cursory scene between Stannis Baratheon and Melisandre.
